summary.intent: Implement comprehensive Microsoft Teams channel improvements to match/exceed the OpenClaw reference implementation, then add agent tools, improve onboarding/docs, and merge main.
summary.outcome: Full Teams channel feature parity achieved with 8 new modules (~1,400 LOC), 70+ tests, Graph API agent tools, improved web UI onboarding, full docs page, and clean merge to main.

repo: msteams crate lives at crates/msteams/src/ with separate modules per concern (auth, outbound, graph, plugin, config, state, activity)
Channel descriptors are registered in crates/channels/src/plugin.rs and must have supports_* flags kept in sync with actual capability
RedactedConfig uses a hardcoded field count that must be manually updated when new config fields are added — a footgun
Agent tools are registered in crates/gateway/src/server.rs and need a matching module in crates/gateway/src/
Per-team/per-channel config overrides use nested TOML: teams.<teamId>.channels.<channelId>
Default offered channels list is in crates/config/src/schema.rs and has a test asserting exact channel set
std::sync::RwLock guards cannot be held across .await points — must be scoped and dropped before any async call

code.finding: RedactedConfig field count was hardcoded as 9 but actual always-present field count was 14 — would cause runtime serialization panics
RwLockWriteGuard on welcomed_conversations was held across .await, making the future non-Send and breaking tokio::spawn
Test for splits_at_space was wrong: 'word1 word2' is exactly 11 chars so rfind splits at space between word1 and word2, not after word2
Teams webhook must return 202 immediately and process in tokio::spawn to avoid Teams retrying on slow LLM responses (causing duplicate messages)
OData search queries need injection protection; ConsistencyLevel: eventual header required for $search on Graph API
MsTeamsOutbound is pub(crate) so edit/delete must be exposed via public delegation methods on MsTeamsPlugin rather than direct outbound access

workflow: Large multi-file Rust implementations benefit from writing all new files in parallel then fixing compilation errors sequentially
Clippy --deny warnings catches unwrap_used in tests, field_reassign_with_default, and collapsible_if — worth running after every major write
Using a subagent to fix all clippy warnings in bulk is more efficient than fixing them one by one in the main context
Commit work before merging main to avoid losing changes — never merge with a dirty working tree
pub(crate) methods on internal structs that are part of the API surface but not yet called should get #[allow(dead_code)] to keep clippy clean

friction: RedactedConfig field count was a silent bug that required manual counting — no compile-time enforcement
std::sync::RwLock non-Send issue only surfaced at httpd compilation, not msteams crate compilation — late discovery
jsonwebtoken was not in workspace deps and had to be added manually to both workspace Cargo.toml and crate Cargo.toml
MsTeamsOutbound being pub(crate) required adding delegation methods on MsTeamsPlugin rather than using it directly from the gateway crate
Clippy had many issues (unwrap_used, collapsible_if, field_reassign_with_default) requiring a dedicated subagent pass to clean up
Multiple rounds of compilation errors before httpd crate was clean (Send bound, visibility issues)
open_items: Localization of Teams strings into fr/zh locale files — intentionally deferred as all channels have this gap
E2E Playwright tests for Teams channel setup — no channel currently has these
Health probe enrichment for Teams beyond connection state + conversation count
Poll vote tracking persistence — polls use in-memory store only, votes lost on restart
Conversation reference store persistence — proactive messaging conversation references are in-memory only
Wiring search/pin/member-info results back into the agent context window as structured data rather than raw JSON strings
